name: shoelace
description: A forward-thinking library of Web Components. 50+ components for building modern UIs.
compatibilityJustification: Shoelace uses Lit internally but does not publish LessJS SSR metadata. All components are client-only.

tags.ssrSnapshot: Infinity

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the alert is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the alert, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the alert's open state.

attributes.name: closable
attributes.description: Enables a close button that allows the user to dismiss the alert.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The alert's theme variant.

attributes.name: duration
attributes.description:
The length of time, in milliseconds, the alert will show before closing itself. If the user interacts with
the alert before it closes (e.g. moves the mouse over it), the timer will restart. Defaults to `Infinity`, meaning
the alert will not close on its own.

attributes.name: countdown
attributes.description:
Enables a countdown that indicates the remaining time the alert will be displayed.
Typically used to indicate the remaining time before a whole app refresh.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the alert opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the alert opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the alert closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the alert closes and all animations are complete.

slots.description: The alert's main content.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: An icon to show in the alert. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description: The path to the image to load.

attributes.name: alt
attributes.description: A description of the image used by assistive devices.

attributes.name: play
attributes.description: Plays the animation. When this attribute is remove, the animation will pause.

events.name: sl-load
events.description: Emitted when the image loads successfully.

events.name: sl-error
events.description: Emitted when the image fails to load.

slots.name: play-icon
slots.description: Optional play icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

slots.name: pause-icon
slots.description: Optional pause icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

attributes.name: image
attributes.description: The image source to use for the avatar.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: A label to use to describe the avatar to assistive devices.

attributes.name: initials
attributes.description: Initials to use as a fallback when no image is available (1-2 characters max recommended).

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description: Indicates how the browser should load the image.

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description: The shape of the avatar.

events.name: sl-error
events.description: The image could not be loaded. This may because of an invalid URL, a temporary network condition, or some unknown cause.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: The default icon to use when no image or initials are present. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The badge's theme variant.

attributes.name: pill
attributes.description: Draws a pill-style badge with rounded edges.

attributes.name: pulse
attributes.description: Makes the badge pulsate to draw attention.

slots.description: The badge's content.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The button's theme variant.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The button's size.

attributes.name: caret
attributes.description: Draws the button with a caret. Used to indicate that the button triggers a dropdown menu or similar behavior.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the button.

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description: Draws the button in a loading state.

attributes.name: outline
attributes.description: Draws an outlined button.

attributes.name: pill
attributes.description: Draws a pill-style button with rounded edges.

attributes.name: circle
attributes.description:
Draws a circular icon button. When this attribute is present, the button expects a single `<sl-icon>` in the
default slot.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
The type of button. Note that the default value is `button` instead of `submit`, which is opposite of how native
`<button>` elements behave. When the type is `submit`, the button will submit the surrounding form.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The name of the button, submitted as a name/value pair with form data, but only when this button is the submitter.
This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The value of the button, submitted as a pair with the button's name as part of the form data, but only when this
button is the submitter. This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: When set, the underlying button will be rendered as an `<a>` with this `href` instead of a `<button>`.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is present.

attributes.name: rel
attributes.description:
When using `href`, this attribute will map to the underlying link's `rel` attribute. Unlike regular links, the
default is `noreferrer noopener` to prevent security exploits. However, if you're using `target` to point to a
specific tab/window, this will prevent that from working correctly. You can remove or change the default value by
setting the attribute to an empty string or a value of your choice, respectively.

attributes.name: download
attributes.description: Tells the browser to download the linked file as this filename. Only used when `href` is present.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
The "form owner" to associate the button with. If omitted, the closest containing form will be used instead. The
value of this attribute must be an id of a form in the same document or shadow root as the button.

attributes.name: formaction
attributes.description: Used to override the form owner's `action` attribute.

attributes.name: formenctype
attributes.description: Used to override the form owner's `enctype` attribute.

attributes.name: formmethod
attributes.description: Used to override the form owner's `method` attribute.

attributes.name: formnovalidate
attributes.description: Used to override the form owner's `novalidate` attribute.

attributes.name: formtarget
attributes.description: Used to override the form owner's `target` attribute.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the button loses focus.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the button gains focus.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.description: The button's label.

slots.name: prefix
slots.description: A presentational prefix icon or similar element.

slots.name: suffix
slots.description: A presentational suffix icon or similar element.
The card's main content.

slots.name: header
slots.description: An optional header for the card.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: An optional footer for the card.

slots.name: image
slots.description: An optional image to render at the start of the card.

tags.ssrSnapshot: sl-carousel

attributes.name: loop
attributes.description: When set, allows the user to navigate the carousel in the same direction indefinitely.

attributes.name: navigation
attributes.description: When set, show the carousel's navigation.

attributes.name: pagination
attributes.description: When set, show the carousel's pagination indicators.

attributes.name: autoplay
attributes.description: When set, the slides will scroll automatically when the user is not interacting with them.

attributes.name: autoplay-interval
attributes.description: Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, between each automatic scroll.

attributes.name: slides-per-page
attributes.description: Specifies how many slides should be shown at a given time.

attributes.name: slides-per-move
attributes.description:
Specifies the number of slides the carousel will advance when scrolling, useful when specifying a `slides-per-page`
greater than one. It can't be higher than `slides-per-page`.

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description: Specifies the orientation in which the carousel will lay out.

attributes.name: mouse-dragging
attributes.description: When set, it is possible to scroll through the slides by dragging them with the mouse.

events.name: sl-slide-change
events.description: Emitted when the active slide changes.

slots.description: The carousel's main content, one or more `<sl-carousel-item>` elements.

slots.name: next-icon
slots.description: Optional next icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

slots.name: previous-icon
slots.description: Optional previous icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The checkbox's size.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the checkbox.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: Draws the checkbox in a checked state.

attributes.name: indeterminate
attributes.description:
Draws the checkbox in an indeterminate state. This is usually applied to checkboxes that represents a "select
all/none" behavior when associated checkboxes have a mix of checked and unchecked states.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Makes the checkbox a required field.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The checkbox's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the checkbox loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the checked state changes.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the checkbox gains focus.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the checkbox receives input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.description: The checkbox's label.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the checkbox. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

tags.ssrSnapshot: hex

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The current value of the color picker. The value's format will vary based the `format` attribute. To get the value
in a specific format, use the `getFormattedValue()` method. The value is submitted as a name/value pair with form
data.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The color picker's label. This will not be displayed, but it will be announced by assistive devices. If you need to
display HTML, you can use the `label` slot` instead.

attributes.name: format
attributes.description:
The format to use. If opacity is enabled, these will translate to HEXA, RGBA, HSLA, and HSVA respectively. The color
picker will accept user input in any format (including CSS color names) and convert it to the desired format.

attributes.name: inline
attributes.description: Renders the color picker inline rather than in a dropdown.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Determines the size of the color picker's trigger. This has no effect on inline color pickers.

attributes.name: no-format-toggle
attributes.description: Removes the button that lets users toggle between format.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the form control, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the color picker.

attributes.name: hoist
attributes.description:
Enable this option to prevent the panel from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

attributes.name: opacity
attributes.description: Shows the opacity slider. Enabling this will cause the formatted value to be HEXA, RGBA, or HSLA.

attributes.name: uppercase
attributes.description: By default, values are lowercase. With this attribute, values will be uppercase instead.

attributes.name: swatches
attributes.description:
One or more predefined color swatches to display as presets in the color picker. Can include any format the color
picker can parse, including HEX(A), RGB(A), HSL(A), HSV(A), and CSS color names. Each color must be separated by a
semicolon (`;`). Alternatively, you can pass an array of color values to this property using JavaScript.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Makes the color picker a required field.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the color picker loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the color picker's value changes.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the color picker receives focus.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the color picker receives input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The color picker's form label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

tags.ssrSnapshot: Details

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the details is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the details, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the details' open state.

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description: The summary to show in the header. If you need to display HTML, use the `summary` slot instead.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the details so it can't be toggled.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the details opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the details opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the details closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the details closes and all animations are complete.

slots.description: The details' main content.

slots.name: summary
slots.description: The details' summary. Alternatively, you can use the `summary` attribute.

slots.name: expand-icon
slots.description: Optional expand icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

slots.name: collapse-icon
slots.description: Optional collapse icon to use instead of the default. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

tags.ssrSnapshot: Dialog

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the dialog is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dialog, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dialog's open state.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The dialog's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label even when using
`no-header`, as it is required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: no-header
attributes.description:
Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the dialog.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the dialog opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the dialog opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the dialog closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the dialog closes and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-initial-focus
events.description: Emitted when the dialog opens and is ready to receive focus. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent focusing and allow you to set it on a different element, such as an input.

events.name: sl-request-close
events.description: Emitted when the user attempts to close the dialog by clicking the close button, clicking the overlay, or pressing escape. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will keep the dialog open. Avoid using this unless closing the dialog will result in destructive behavior such as data loss.

slots.description: The dialog's main content.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The dialog's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: header-actions
slots.description: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<sl-icon-button>`.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: The dialog's footer, usually one or more buttons representing various options.

attributes.name: vertical
attributes.description: Draws the divider in a vertical orientation.

tags.ssrSnapshot: Drawer

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the drawer is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the drawer, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the drawer's open state.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The drawer's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label even when using
`no-header`, as it is required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: The direction from which the drawer will open.

attributes.name: contained
attributes.description:
By default, the drawer slides out of its containing block (usually the viewport). To make the drawer slide out of
its parent element, set this attribute and add `position: relative` to the parent.

attributes.name: no-header
attributes.description:
Removes the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the drawer.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the drawer opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the drawer opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the drawer closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the drawer closes and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-initial-focus
events.description: Emitted when the drawer opens and is ready to receive focus. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent focusing and allow you to set it on a different element, such as an input.

events.name: sl-request-close
events.description: Emitted when the user attempts to close the drawer by clicking the close button, clicking the overlay, or pressing escape. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will keep the drawer open. Avoid using this unless closing the drawer will result in destructive behavior such as data loss.

slots.description: The drawer's main content.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The drawer's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: header-actions
slots.description: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<sl-icon-button>`.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: The drawer's footer, usually one or more buttons representing various options.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the dropdown is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dropdown, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dropdown's open state.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
The preferred placement of the dropdown panel. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the panel
inside of the viewport.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the dropdown so the panel will not open.

attributes.name: stay-open-on-select
attributes.description:
By default, the dropdown is closed when an item is selected. This attribute will keep it open instead. Useful for
dropdowns that allow for multiple interactions.

attributes.name: distance
attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its trigger.

attributes.name: skidding
attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its trigger.

attributes.name: hoist
attributes.description:
Enable this option to prevent the panel from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

attributes.name: sync
attributes.description: Syncs the popup width or height to that of the trigger element.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the dropdown opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the dropdown opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the dropdown closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the dropdown closes and all animations are complete.

slots.description: The dropdown's main content.

slots.name: trigger
slots.description: The dropdown's trigger, usually a `<sl-button>` element.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the icon to draw. Available names depend on the icon library being used.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
An external URL of an SVG file. Be sure you trust the content you are including, as it will be executed as code and
can result in XSS attacks.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
An alternate description to use for assistive devices. If omitted, the icon will be considered presentational and
ignored by assistive devices.

attributes.name: library
attributes.description: The name of a registered custom icon library.

events.name: sl-load
events.description: Emitted when the icon has loaded. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit.

events.name: sl-error
events.description: Emitted when the icon fails to load due to an error. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the icon to draw. Available names depend on the icon library being used.

attributes.name: library
attributes.description: The name of a registered custom icon library.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
An external URL of an SVG file. Be sure you trust the content you are including, as it will be executed as code and
can result in XSS attacks.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: When set, the underlying button will be rendered as an `<a>` with this `href` instead of a `<button>`.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is set.

attributes.name: download
attributes.description: Tells the browser to download the linked file as this filename. Only used when `href` is set.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
A description that gets read by assistive devices. For optimal accessibility, you should always include a label
that describes what the icon button does.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the button.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the icon button loses focus.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the icon button gains focus.

attributes.name: position
attributes.description: The position of the divider as a percentage.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the position changes.

slots.name: before
slots.description: The before image, an `` or `

slots.name: after
slots.description: The after image, an `` or `

slots.name: handle
slots.description: The icon used inside the handle.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
The type of input. Works the same as a native `<input>` element, but only a subset of types are supported. Defaults
to `text`.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the input, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the input, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The input's size.

attributes.name: filled
attributes.description: Draws a filled input.

attributes.name: pill
attributes.description: Draws a pill-style input with rounded edges.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The input's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The input's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

attributes.name: clearable
attributes.description: Adds a clear button when the input is not empty.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the input.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description: Makes the input readonly.

attributes.name: password-toggle
attributes.description: Adds a button to toggle the password's visibility. Only applies to password types.

attributes.name: password-visible
attributes.description: Determines whether or not the password is currently visible. Only applies to password input types.

attributes.name: no-spin-buttons
attributes.description: Hides the browser's built-in increment/decrement spin buttons for number inputs.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Makes the input a required field.

attributes.name: pattern
attributes.description: A regular expression pattern to validate input against.

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

attributes.name: maxlength
attributes.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: The input's minimum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: The input's maximum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

attributes.name: step
attributes.description:
Specifies the granularity that the value must adhere to, or the special value `any` which means no stepping is
implied, allowing any numeric value. Only applies to date and number input types.

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

attributes.name: enterkeyhint
attributes.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description: Enables spell checking on the input.

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

events.name: sl-clear
events.description: Emitted when the clear button is activated.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The input's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: prefix
slots.description: Used to prepend a presentational icon or similar element to the input.

slots.name: suffix
slots.description: Used to append a presentational icon or similar element to the input.

slots.name: clear-icon
slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default clear icon.

slots.name: show-password-icon
slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default show password icon.

slots.name: hide-password-icon
slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default hide password icon.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

events.name: sl-select
events.description: Emitted when a menu item is selected.

slots.description: The menu's content, including menu items, menu labels, and dividers.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The type of menu item to render. To use `checked`, this value must be set to `checkbox`.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: Draws the item in a checked state.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: A unique value to store in the menu item. This can be used as a way to identify menu items when selected.

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description: Draws the menu item in a loading state.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Draws the menu item in a disabled state, preventing selection.

slots.description: The menu item's label.

slots.name: prefix
slots.description: Used to prepend an icon or similar element to the menu item.

slots.name: suffix
slots.description: Used to append an icon or similar element to the menu item.

slots.name: submenu
slots.description: Used to denote a nested menu.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current progress as a percentage, 0 to 100.

attributes.name: indeterminate
attributes.description: When true, percentage is ignored, the label is hidden, and the progress bar is drawn in an indeterminate state.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: A custom label for assistive devices.

slots.description: A label to show inside the progress indicator.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The radio's value. When selected, the radio group will receive this value.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
The radio's size. When used inside a radio group, the size will be determined by the radio group's size so this
attribute can typically be omitted.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the radio.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

slots.description: The radio's label.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot
instead.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The radio groups's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The radio group's size. This size will be applied to all child radios and radio buttons.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Ensures a child radio is checked before allowing the containing form to submit.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the radio group's selected value changes.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the radio group receives user input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.description: The default slot where `<sl-radio>` or `<sl-radio-button>` elements are placed.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the radio group. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

tags.ssrSnapshot: 50

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the range, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the range, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The range's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The range's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the help-text slot instead.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the range.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: The minimum acceptable value of the range.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: The maximum acceptable value of the range.

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: The interval at which the range will increase and decrease.

attributes.name: tooltip
attributes.description: The preferred placement of the range's tooltip.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The range's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: A label that describes the rating to assistive devices.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current rating.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: The highest rating to show.

attributes.name: precision
attributes.description:
The precision at which the rating will increase and decrease. For example, to allow half-star ratings, set this
attribute to `0.5`.

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description: Makes the rating readonly.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the rating.

attributes.name: getSymbol
attributes.description:
A function that customizes the symbol to be rendered. The first and only argument is the rating's current value.
The function should return a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at the specified value. Works
well with `<sl-icon>` elements.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the rating's value changes.

events.name: sl-hover
events.description: Emitted when the user hovers over a value. The `phase` property indicates when hovering starts, moves to a new value, or ends. The `value` property tells what the rating's value would be if the user were to commit to the hovered value.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The select's size.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the select is empty.

attributes.name: multiple
attributes.description: Allows more than one option to be selected.

attributes.name: max-options-visible
attributes.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the select control.

attributes.name: clearable
attributes.description: Adds a clear button when the select is not empty.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the select is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the menu, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the select's open state.

attributes.name: hoist
attributes.description:
Enable this option to prevent the listbox from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

attributes.name: filled
attributes.description: Draws a filled select.

attributes.name: pill
attributes.description: Draws a pill-style select with rounded edges.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The select's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
The preferred placement of the select's menu. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the listbox
inside of the viewport.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The select's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: The select's required attribute.

attributes.name: getTag
attributes.type: (option: SlOption, index: number) => TemplateResult | string | HTMLElement
attributes.description:
A function that customizes the tags to be rendered when multiple=true. The first argument is the option, the second
is the current tag's index.  The function should return either a Lit TemplateResult or a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at
the specified value.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the control's value changes.

events.name: sl-clear
events.description: Emitted when the control's value is cleared.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the select's menu opens.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the select's menu opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the select's menu closes.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the select's menu closes and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.description: The listbox options. Must be `<sl-option>` elements. You can use `<sl-divider>` to group items visually.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The input's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: prefix
slots.description: Used to prepend a presentational icon or similar element to the combobox.

slots.name: suffix
slots.description: Used to append a presentational icon or similar element to the combobox.

slots.name: clear-icon
slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default clear icon.

slots.name: expand-icon
slots.description: The icon to show when the control is expanded and collapsed. Rotates on open and close.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

attributes.name: effect
attributes.description: Determines which effect the skeleton will use.

attributes.name: position
attributes.description:
The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge as a percentage 0-100. Defaults to 50% of the
container's initial size.

attributes.name: position-in-pixels
attributes.description: The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge in pixels.

attributes.name: vertical
attributes.description: Draws the split panel in a vertical orientation with the start and end panels stacked.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables resizing. Note that the position may still change as a result of resizing the host element.

attributes.name: primary
attributes.description:
If no primary panel is designated, both panels will resize proportionally when the host element is resized. If a
primary panel is designated, it will maintain its size and the other panel will grow or shrink as needed when the
host element is resized.

attributes.name: snap
attributes.description:
Either one or more space-separated values at which the divider should snap, in pixels, percentages, or repeat expressions e.g. `'100px 50% 500px' or `repeat(50%) 10px`,
or a function which takes in a `SnapFunctionParams`, and returns a position to snap to, e.g. `({ pos }) => Math.round(pos / 8) * 8`.

attributes.name: snap-threshold
attributes.description: How close the divider must be to a snap point until snapping occurs.

events.name: sl-reposition
events.description: Emitted when the divider's position changes.

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content to place in the start panel.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content to place in the end panel.

slots.name: divider
slots.description: The divider. Useful for slotting in a custom icon that renders as a handle.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The switch's size.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the switch.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: Draws the switch in a checked state.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Makes the switch a required field.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The switch's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when the control's checked state changes.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.description: The switch's label.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the switch. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

attributes.name: panel
attributes.description: The name of the tab panel this tab is associated with. The panel must be located in the same tab group.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description: Draws the tab in an active state.

attributes.name: closable
attributes.description: Makes the tab closable and shows a close button.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the tab and prevents selection.

events.name: sl-close
events.description: Emitted when the tab is closable and the close button is activated.

slots.description: The tab's label.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: The placement of the tabs.

attributes.name: activation
attributes.description:
When set to auto, navigating tabs with the arrow keys will instantly show the corresponding tab panel. When set to
manual, the tab will receive focus but will not show until the user presses spacebar or enter.

attributes.name: no-scroll-controls
attributes.description: Disables the scroll arrows that appear when tabs overflow.

attributes.name: fixed-scroll-controls
attributes.description: Prevent scroll buttons from being hidden when inactive.

events.name: sl-tab-show
events.description: Emitted when a tab is shown.

events.name: sl-tab-hide
events.description: Emitted when a tab is hidden.

slots.description: Used for grouping tab panels in the tab group. Must be `<sl-tab-panel>` elements.

slots.name: nav
slots.description: Used for grouping tabs in the tab group. Must be `<sl-tab>` elements.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The tab panel's name.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description: When true, the tab panel will be shown.

slots.description: The tab panel's content.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The tag's theme variant.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The tag's size.

attributes.name: pill
attributes.description: Draws a pill-style tag with rounded edges.

attributes.name: removable
attributes.description: Makes the tag removable and shows a remove button.

events.name: sl-remove
events.description: Emitted when the remove button is activated.

slots.description: The tag's content.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the textarea, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The current value of the textarea, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The textarea's size.

attributes.name: filled
attributes.description: Draws a filled textarea.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The textarea's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

attributes.name: help-text
attributes.description: The textarea's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

attributes.name: rows
attributes.description: The number of rows to display by default.

attributes.name: resize
attributes.description: Controls how the textarea can be resized.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the textarea.

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description: Makes the textarea readonly.

attributes.name: form
attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Makes the textarea a required field.

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

attributes.name: maxlength
attributes.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

attributes.name: enterkeyhint
attributes.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description: Enables spell checking on the textarea.

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

events.name: sl-blur
events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.

events.name: sl-change
events.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user.

events.name: sl-focus
events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.

events.name: sl-input
events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.

events.name: sl-invalid
events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The textarea's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: help-text
slots.description: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.

tags.ssrSnapshot: Tooltip content

attributes.name: content
attributes.description: The tooltip's content. If you need to display HTML, use the `content` slot instead.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
The preferred placement of the tooltip. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the tooltip
inside of the viewport.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the tooltip so it won't show when triggered.

attributes.name: distance
attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip away from its target.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Indicates whether or not the tooltip is open. You can use this in lieu of the show/hide methods.

attributes.name: skidding
attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip along its target.

attributes.name: trigger
attributes.description:
Controls how the tooltip is activated. Possible options include `click`, `hover`, `focus`, and `manual`. Multiple
options can be passed by separating them with a space. When manual is used, the tooltip must be activated
programmatically.

attributes.name: hoist
attributes.description:
Enable this option to prevent the tooltip from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|hidden|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all,
scenarios.

events.name: sl-show
events.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to show.

events.name: sl-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the tooltip has shown and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-hide
events.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to hide.

events.name: sl-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the tooltip has hidden and all animations are complete.

slots.description: The tooltip's target element. Avoid slotting in more than one element, as subsequent ones will be ignored.

slots.name: content
slots.description: The content to render in the tooltip. Alternatively, you can use the `content` attribute.

attributes.name: selection
attributes.description:
The selection behavior of the tree. Single selection allows only one node to be selected at a time. Multiple
displays checkboxes and allows more than one node to be selected. Leaf allows only leaf nodes to be selected.

events.name: sl-selection-change
events.description: Emitted when a tree item is selected or deselected.

slots.description: The default slot.

slots.name: expand-icon
slots.description: The icon to show when the tree item is expanded. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

slots.name: collapse-icon
slots.description: The icon to show when the tree item is collapsed. Works best with `<sl-icon>`.

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description: Expands the tree item.

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description: Draws the tree item in a selected state.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the tree item.

attributes.name: lazy
attributes.description: Enables lazy loading behavior.

events.name: sl-expand
events.description: Emitted when the tree item expands.

events.name: sl-after-expand
events.description: Emitted after the tree item expands and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-collapse
events.description: Emitted when the tree item collapses.

events.name: sl-after-collapse
events.description: Emitted after the tree item collapses and all animations are complete.

events.name: sl-lazy-change
events.description: Emitted when the tree item's lazy state changes.

events.name: sl-lazy-load
events.description: Emitted when a lazy item is selected. Use this event to asynchronously load data and append items to the tree before expanding. After appending new items, remove the `lazy` attribute to remove the loading state and update the tree.

slots.description: The default slot.

slots.name: expand-icon
slots.description: The icon to show when the tree item is expanded.

slots.name: collapse-icon
slots.description: The icon to show when the tree item is collapsed.

warnings: This package has no SSR metadata. It will render on the client only.
